events.name: bash

input.description: Fetch PR review comments with IDs

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Good call. Concrete fix: replace `formatOutput(data: any)` with a constrained generic so callers keep type-safety without using `any`, e.g. `export function formatOutput<T extends Record<string, unknown> | unknown[]>(data: T): string { return JSON.stringify(data) }`. If this function should accept primitives too, use `T = unknown` and keep the generic return as `string`.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Agreed. Concrete fix: wrap `JSON.parse` in a safe parser and return a result object instead of throwing. Example shape: `type ParseResult = { ok: true; data: unknown } | { ok: false; error: string }`; then `try { return { ok: true, data: JSON.parse(raw) } } catch (err) { return { ok: false, error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Invalid JSON" } }`. This keeps parse failures explicit and non-throwing for callers.

events.name: github_add_reply_to_pull_request_comment

input.body: Makes sense. Concrete fix: implement real schema validation and return a typed result. For example, accept a validator function: `validateSchema(data: unknown, validate: (input: unknown) => boolean): boolean { return validate(data) }`, or if using a schema library (AJV/Zod), run validation and return `{ ok: boolean; errors?: string[] }` so callers can inspect why validation failed.

events.content:
Done — I replied to all unresolved review threads on PR `#432` with concrete fix suggestions, and only those unresolved threads.

Replied threads:
- `#discussion_r2879420356` (typed replacement for `any` in `formatOutput`)
- `#discussion_r2879420456` (safe `JSON.parse` error-handling pattern)
- `#discussion_r2879420583` (real schema validation approach with typed result)

No code was pushed or modified.
